expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves banding requirements, style constraints, and rendering assumptions before scenario drafting
expectations.evidence: The output explicitly states the benchmark focus and enumerates the three items to be preserved before scenario drafting: "This benchmark checks that context intake captures and preserves, before any scenario drafting, the requirements/assumptions for: 1) Banding requirements ... 2) Style constraints ... 3) Rendering assumptions ..." It also restates the specific Modern Grid limitations/requirements from the Jira evidence (rows only, cannot format colors, cannot do columns, header-based banding, dashboards/readability/parity with Report).

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: The result is framed as "Phase 1 (Context intake) Contract Check" and "Primary phase under test: phase1". It describes Phase 1 responsibilities (spawn-only orchestration, spawn manifest, spawn/--post loop) and explicitly avoids later phases: execution_notes.md says "Kept assessment aligned to phase1 only; did not draft scenarios or invoke later-phase expectations."
